sanhedrin-43a-yeshu-trial.vilna_standard: The baraita naming Yeshu ha-Notzri and describing his trial and execution was omitted entirely from the standard Vilna Shas and many pre-modern printings.
sanhedrin-43a-yeshu-trial.witness_reading: Munich 95 and Bomberg retain a baraita: Yeshu ha-Notzri was hanged on the eve of Passover; the gemara then asks whether he might have had zechut (legal merit) for acquittal — a procedural question inside the laws of mesis (enticer), not a creed about Christianity.
sanhedrin-43a-yeshu-trial.plain_english: The Talmud records a trial-and-execution narrative about a figure called Yeshu the Nazarene, then debates whether the court could have acquitted him on technical grounds. Jewish tradition treats this as a legal-historical report in context, not as normative theology about Jesus.
sanhedrin-43a-yeshu-trial.sefaria_note: Sefaria’s Hebrew gemara often restores this passage; English may paraphrase softly. Compare their text to this build and to Instone-Brewer.

sanhedrin-67a-ben-stada.vilna_standard: Passage linking ben Stada / ben Pandera to execution for mesis was cut from Vilna-era print.
sanhedrin-67a-ben-stada.witness_reading: Manuscripts report the execution of ben Stada from Lud; amoraic discussion plays on the names ben Stada and ben Pandera — traditionally cross-read with Yeshu loci.
sanhedrin-67a-ben-stada.plain_english: A reported capital case for ‘enticing’ (mesis), with wordplay on names later linked in rabbinic literature to the same figure cluster as Sanhedrin 43a.
sanhedrin-67a-ben-stada.sefaria_note: Restoration varies by layer; read English alongside Munich-based Hebrew here.

gittin-57a-yeshu-gehinnom.vilna_standard: Aggadic block naming Yeshu ha-Notzri among those punished in Gehinnom was removed from censored print.
gittin-57a-yeshu-gehinnom.witness_reading: Onkelos the convert summons Yeshu ha-Notzri from Gehinnom in an aggadic chain about Roman oppressors — homiletical narrative tied to the destruction era, not a halachic ruling.
gittin-57a-yeshu-gehinnom.plain_english: A story-world vignette: a named Yeshu the Nazarene appears in Gehinnom in a polemical aggadah about empire and punishment. It is legend in context, not law.
gittin-57a-yeshu-gehinnom.sefaria_note: English translations may tone down polemical edges; Hebrew witness text is the check.

sanhedrin-107b-yeshu-student.vilna_standard: Biographical aggadah placing Yeshu as a student of R. Yehoshua ben Perahiah was censored from standard print.
sanhedrin-107b-yeshu-student.witness_reading: Story of a student (named Yeshu in uncensored witnesses) who misunderstood his teacher’s hospitality and was expelled — moralistic aggadah with debated historicity.
sanhedrin-107b-yeshu-student.plain_english: A cautionary tale about a brilliant but misguided student; later tradition names him Yeshu. Read as narrative ethics, not biography.
sanhedrin-107b-yeshu-student.sefaria_note: Name and details often absent in censored editions; Sefaria may restore Hebrew but English summaries vary.

sotah-47a-yeshu-student.vilna_standard: Parallel version of the student narrative — same censorship profile as Sanhedrin 107b.
sotah-47a-yeshu-student.witness_reading: Duplicate aggadic tradition; uncensored witnesses preserve the Yeshu naming.
sotah-47a-yeshu-student.plain_english: Same story-line as 107b in a parallel sugya — useful for textual witness comparison.
sotah-47a-yeshu-student.sefaria_note: Compare both dapim; do not assume English on one replaces the other.

shabbat-104b-ben-stada.vilna_standard: ben Stada material in the sorcery context was suppressed in Vilna print.
shabbat-104b-ben-stada.witness_reading: Narrative in a magic/sorcery discussion preserving ben Stada / ben Pandera name-play tied to Sanhedrin 67a.
shabbat-104b-ben-stada.plain_english: Legal-aggadic overlap between sorcery law and the ben Stada name traditions.
shabbat-104b-ben-stada.sefaria_note: Among the easier passages to miss in English-only browsing; Hebrew restoration matters.

avodah-zarah-2a-goyim-euphemism.vilna_standard: Vilna Rashi routinely reads עובדי כוכבים (idol-worshippers) where earlier witnesses have גוים (gentiles/nations).
avodah-zarah-2a-goyim-euphemism.witness_reading: Munich/Bomberg Rashi on the opening mishnah uses גוים — the direct word Rashi wrote before Christian censorship.
avodah-zarah-2a-goyim-euphemism.plain_english: Not a change in halacha but in vocabulary: ‘gentiles’ was replaced by the safer phrase ‘idol-worshippers’ in print. The legal sugya is about commerce before pagan festivals.
avodah-zarah-2a-goyim-euphemism.sefaria_note: Sefaria English often follows Vilna Rashi wording — you may read ‘idol-worshippers’ where witnesses say ‘gentiles’.

berakhot-12a-akum-rashi.vilna_standard: Vilna Rashi uses עכו"ם (akum — censor acronym for ‘idol-worshippers’) instead of גוים or מין (sectarians) in Shema commentary.
berakhot-12a-akum-rashi.witness_reading: Earlier Rashi witnesses read goyim or minim where Vilna has the opaque acronym עכו"ם.
berakhot-12a-akum-rashi.plain_english: A censorship swap in commentary only: an invented acronym hides who Rashi was talking about in a legal reading of Shema.
berakhot-12a-akum-rashi.sefaria_note: English will often say ‘idolaters’ or transliterate akum — it will not flag that Rashi’s word was different.

bava-metzia-59b-oven-of-achnai.vilna_standard: No meaningful censorship — standard Vilna text matches witnesses.
bava-metzia-59b-oven-of-achnai.witness_reading: Same as Vilna: Oven of Akhnai; Rabbi Eliezer vs sages; bat kol rejected — ‘It is not in heaven.’
bava-metzia-59b-oven-of-achnai.plain_english: Famous dispute: human rabbinic majority outweighs a heavenly voice. Included as a baseline: rich argument preserved everywhere, not a suppression locus.
bava-metzia-59b-oven-of-achnai.sefaria_note: Sefaria English is a reliable reading aid here.

eruvin-13b-rabbi-meir.vilna_standard: No censorship — dispute about why halacha does not follow Rabbi Meir is on the record in all prints.
eruvin-13b-rabbi-meir.witness_reading: Same: peers could not fully grasp Rabbi Meir’s reasoning; meta-machloket on authority.
eruvin-13b-rabbi-meir.plain_english: Argument about limits of following a genius sage — preserved openly, not suppressed.
eruvin-13b-rabbi-meir.sefaria_note: Safe to use Sefaria English for overview.

sanhedrin-37a-adam-single.vilna_standard: Universal dignity teaching (‘Adam was created alone…’) appears in all standard editions.
sanhedrin-37a-adam-single.witness_reading: Identical witness stream — teaching on human equality and murder accountability.
sanhedrin-37a-adam-single.plain_english: Because God created one Adam, no person may say ‘my lineage is greater than yours’ — classic aggadah, uncensored.
sanhedrin-37a-adam-single.sefaria_note: English translations align well; included as non-censorship contrast.

global-akum-commentary.vilna_standard: Roughly ~100 places in Rashi/Tosafot where Vilna has עכו"ם instead of גוים or related direct terms.
global-akum-commentary.witness_reading: Pattern across commentary layers: censor acronym replaces plain ‘gentiles’ in manuscript Rashi/Tosafot.
global-akum-commentary.plain_english: 100 indexed commentary rows now have English witness notes — open the Audit list (audit.html) to browse every hit by tractate and daf, or open any flagged daf for inline notes.
global-akum-commentary.sefaria_note: Default Sefaria commentary export reflects Vilna; use audit.html + per-daf notes here instead of trusting English commentary alone.
